name: US.TradeServCatTotal
Title: Services (BPM6): Exports and imports by service category, trading partner world, annual
Keywords: services, exports, imports, trade, transport, travel, sector, category
Category: International trade in goods and services
Author: UNCTAD
Notes:
Description:

This table presents annual statistics on international trade in services of individual economies by some 100 service categories for trade-partner “World total”. It further contains aggregated figures for various groups of economies for selected principal service categories. The data presented are the result of the common work of UNCTAD and the World Trade Organization (WTO).  

This table includes only trade-partner “World total” statistics, if available.  
Another UNCTADstat table - “Services (BPM6): Exports and imports by service-category and by trade-partner, annual “ - covers individual economies’ figures for trade in services by bilateral trade-partner, when such figures are available.

The data are shown in five different measures:
- millions of United States dollars
- percentages of the world total
- annual percentage changes (growth rates)
- shares of each service category in total services
- shares in current GDP (gross domestic product) (selected main service categories)

Sources:
UNCTAD–WTO Trade in Services Data Set, based on:
-    Eurostat, online database
-    OECD, OECD.Stat
-    IMF, Balance of Payments Statistics
-    Other international and national sources
-    UNCTAD - WTO estimates.

Terms of use:
Using any data and metadata from this table must include a citation “WTO-UNCTAD Trade in Services Data Set”.

All materials provided on the UNCTADstat website are copyrighted under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 IGO license. The data and metadata may be copied freely, duplicated and further distributed provided that United Nations Trade and Development Data Hub is cited as the source

Notes:
The statistics presented correspond to the concepts and definitions of the IMF Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual, Sixth Edition (BPM6, 2009). Figures reported according to the fifth edition of the Manual (BPM5) have been adjusted to the BPM6 definitions, provided that such adjustment was possible. When possible, the values missing in principal international sources are estimated by using growth rates derived from the data available in national or other international sources.

UNCTAD and WTO may use different definitions for certain groups of economies (“developed” and “developing” economies, for example). Consequently, data for some aggregates will be different from those published by WTO, although the individual-economy data are the same.

BPM6 (paragraph 10.8) explains services and its distinction from goods as follows:
"Services are the result of a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production. However, […] some knowledge-capturing products, such as computer software and other intellectual property products, may be traded separately from their production, like goods. In the balance of payments goods and services account, the valuation of goods includes transport within the exporting economy as well as wholesale and retail services indistinguishably in the price of the goods. Furthermore, the value of some service items includes the values of some goods, in the cases of travel, construction, and government goods and services n.i.e. Some services, particularly manufacturing services, repairs, and freight transport, also relate to goods."

For summary definitions of the main services categories, refer to the item notes attached to the category- labels. The full list of the categories presented can be displayed by clicking on the "CATEGORY" label in the online table view.

For detailed explanations, please refer to Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual, Sixth Edition (BPM6, 2009):
 (https://www.imf.org/external/pubs/ft/bop/2007/pdf/bpm6.pdf ).
and to the Manual on Statistics of International Trade in Services (MSITS, 2010).
 (http://unstats.un.org/unsd/tradeserv/tfsits/manual.htm)
